text: In software development, testing plays a crucial role in ensuring quality. Unit tests check small pieces of code, like individual functions, to make sure they work correctly. Integration tests go further by verifying that different parts of the system work together smoothly. A popular approach is test-driven development, where developers write tests before writing the actual code. This helps them think about requirements first and often leads to cleaner design. Together, these practices build confidence in the software. When tests pass, developers can trust that changes won't break existing features, making the whole process more reliable and efficient.
